title: Iran at the 2026 World Cup: Team Melli Target First Knockout Berth
original_text:
Iran at the 2026 World Cup: Team Melli Target First Knockout Berth
Team card
Status : World Cup 2026 participant
Head coach : Amir Ghalenoei.
Group: G.
Opponents: Belgium, Egypt, New Zealand.
Best World Cup result: Group stage (six appearances; never advanced to the Round of 16).
Key players: Mehdi Taremi, Alireza Jahanbakhsh, Alireza Beiranvand, Saman Ghoddos, Rouzbeh Cheshmi
Iran
Tournament intro
The 2026 FIFA World Cup will feature matches from June 11 to July 19, 2026. The event is co-hosted by the USA, Canada, and Mexico in a record 48-team format. And no doubt, Iran are going to the World Cup for the 7th time and their 4th in a row. They are placed in Group G, alongside Belgium, Egypt, and New Zealand, and will play all three of their group-stage fixtures on the western seaboard of the United States. Amir Ghalenoei is on the verge of breaking new ground on the global stage, and if he can make it to the next round, he will make history. He was appointed to the team again in March 2023 for his second spell, having previously managed the team between 2006 and 2007.
How Iran qualified for World Cup 2026
Iran qualified for the 2026 World Cup after an impressive run in Group A of the AFC Third Round qualifying. They finished top of the group with 23 points, including seven wins, two draws, and a defeat. They got the better of Qatar, Uzbekistan, the UAE, Kyrgyz Republic, and Korea DPR during this process. They confirmed their ticket as early as March 2025 after a 2-2 draw against Uzbekistan in Tehran. With the result, they became the third non-host nation to seal their place at the 2026 finals, behind Japan and New Zealand.
Iran’s World Cup 2026 group
Group G: Belgium (UEFA), Egypt (CAF), Iran (AFC), and New Zealand (OFC).
Belgium: Belgium are no doubt the heavy favourite in this group. They carry remnants of the famous “golden generation” into what is likely a final chapter for several of their most decorated names. The likes of Kevin De Bruyne, Romelu Lukaku, and Thibaut Courtois are heading into the finals. It might be the last time we see these faces at the world event; nevertheless, they bring the much-needed experience for their sides. After failing to make it out of their group at the Qatar 2022 event, the target now is to exceed expectations here. While the game against the Belgians looks very unwinnable for the Iranians, never say never.
Romelu Lukaku
Egypt: Egypt missed out on the 2022 World Cup in Qatar but are now back on the top stage. The combination of Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush in the attack could prove to be the strength of the Pharaohs in this tournament. So, Iran must be aware of the danger these two players bring to the clash. They both have the speed that can help on the counter.
New Zealand: New Zealand are back in the World Cup for the first time since 2010. Although they are the lowest-ranked team (86th in the world by FIFA), they can prove to be a hard nut to crack. Iran will start as favourites, but New Zealand have improved since 2010 and should not be taken lightly.
Group G difficulty assessment
On paper, Iran are among the teams competing for second place behind Belgium, with New Zealand and Egypt both capable of causing problems. A strong result against the All Whites in the opener will be important, and Iran may also need to factor in the best third-placed teams scenario if they miss the top two.
Match schedule (group stage)
DateMatchCity / StadiumLocal kick-off (ET)Group Matchday
Monday, 15 June 2026 Iran vs New Zealand SoFi Stadium, Inglewood (Los Angeles) 9:00 PM MD1
Sunday, 21 June 2026 Belgium vs Iran SoFi Stadium, Inglewood (Los Angeles) 3:00 PM MD2
Friday, 26 June 2026 Egypt vs Iran Lumen Field, Seattle 11:00 PM MD3
All times Eastern Time (ET). Times and venues are confirmed per official announcements; the full knockout schedule will be updated as the tournament progresses.
Squad for World Cup 2026 (official)
Iran’s official 26-man squad was confirmed on 1 June 2026.
Goalkeepers: Alireza Beiranvand, Seyed Hossein Hosseini, Payam Niazmand.
Defenders: Danial Eiri, Ehsan Hajsafi, Saleh Hardani, Hossein Kanaanizadegan, Shoja Khalilzadeh, Milad Mohammadi, Ali Nemati, Ramin Rezaeian.
Midfielders: Rouzbeh Cheshmi, Saeid Ezatolahi, Mehdi Ghaedi, Saman Ghoddos, Mohammad Ghorbani, Alireza Jahanbakhsh, Mohammad Mohebi, Amir Mohammad Razzaghinia, Mehdi Torabi, Aria Yousefi.
Forwards: Ali Alipour, Dennis Dargahi (Eckert), Amirhossein Hosseinzadeh, Mehdi Taremi (C), Shahriar Moghanlou.
Probable starting XI
Predicted shape: 4-2-3-1
Beiranvand; Ramin Rezaeian, Nemati, Khalilzadeh, Milad Mohammadi; Ezatolahi, Cheshmi; Jahanbakhsh, Ghoddos, Ghayedi; Taremi.
Mehdi Taremi
Ghalenoei has been more concerned about the defence of the team, with Ezatolahi and Cheshmi anchoring the middle. Nemati and Khalilzadeh have proved to be a good partner at the back, and we could see the best in the partnership of the two at the World Cup.
Note: this is a predicted XI based on squad information available as of 6 June 2026. Official lineups will be announced prior to each match.
Head coach & star players
Ghalenoei is not new to coaching the Iranian national team. He has managed them before and was impressive in his first reign in 2006–07 before returning to club football. Since returning in 2023, he has maintained that level of respect in the team, and with counter-attack-driven football, he should go far in this tournament.
Star players (mini-cards):
Mehdi Taremi — Forward, Captain: Taremi brings experience and talent to the table. At 33 years old and in his third World Cup, he brings the kind of experienced, composed finishing that no other Iranian player can replicate.
Alireza Jahanbakhsh — Midfielder/Winger: One of the team’s senior leaders brings creativity and a wealth of experience. The 32-year-old has both international and club experience from Europe, which gives him confidence in attack.
Alireza Beiranvand — Goalkeeper: With over 80 caps for the national team, Beiranvand is on the path to becoming the greatest Iranian goalkeeper in history. He has been with the team for so long and has been used to the system.
Saman Ghoddos — Midfielder: Ghoddos has functioned in different capacities over the years. He is composed under pressure, and his passes can unlock tight defence.
Rouzbeh Cheshmi — Midfielder: The engine room of Iran’s midfield. Skilful, talented and disciplined. He offers lots of defensive cover and contributions to the attack.
Rouzbeh Cheshmi
Strengths & weaknesses
Strengths: Iran can take much pride in their defence, and Ghalenoei’s intentionality with the team is something commendable. With direct transitions, a compact shape, and even set-pieces, the team is capable of downing any side in their group.
Weaknesses: While most people might see their weaknesses on the field, they face many off-field issues. Political and logistical distractions can have a huge effect on their performance. No team going to the World Cup is navigating anything close to the diplomatic complexity surrounding Iran's participation. From media scrutiny to political tension.
Best World Cup results
In 1978, Iran made their debut at the World Cup in Argentina, but it managed to pick up just one point in the competition. Their six previous appearances at the 1978, 1998, 2006, 2014, 2018, and 2022 have all ended in the group stage. It remains to be seen whether they can break that jinx this time around.
What to expect / prediction
Iran are targeting a first-ever knockout-stage berth at a World Cup. They won't have this on a platter. But with three or four points in the group, they should make it to the next round. 1xBet offers 1.5 odds on Iran to advance to the next round.
More markets for Iran (group progression, outrights) are available on platforms like 1xBet (18+, gamble responsibly).
FAQ
Is Iran at the 2026 World Cup?
Yes. Iran qualified by winning AFC Third Round qualifying Group A, finishing with 23 points from 10 matches (7W–2D–1L).
Which World Cup group is Iran in?
Iran are in Group G alongside Belgium, Egypt, and New Zealand.
Who is Iran's national team head coach?
Amir Ghalenoei
Who does Iran play in the group stage?
Iran face New Zealand (15 June, Los Angeles), Belgium (21 June, Los Angeles), and Egypt (26 June, Seattle).
